journey.description: Start, resume, or check the health of an architectural journey
journey.prompt: First, check whether docs/journey/journey-state.md exists and read it if it does. If a journey is already in progress, summarize current status and ask what to do next (continue, iterate stressor loop, review health, or establish cadence). If no state file exists, ask: What is the engagement — a new greenfield design, an existing brownfield system, or a live system you need to evolve? Then follow the journey skill workflow.

discover.description: Map paths, actors, and intentions of an existing system
discover.prompt: Use the discover skill. Ask the user: What would you like to discover — system paths, a specific actor's behaviour, how an intention propagates through the system, confidence gaps, or organisational resistance?

stressor.description: Run stressor analysis or apply a compliance pack to an architectural path
stressor.prompt: Use the stressor skill. Ask: Would you like to walk a specific path (evaluating each actor in sequence), run a full stressor analysis to build an impact matrix, or inject a compliance pack such as GDPR? If walking a path, ask which path to start with.

adr.prompt: Use the adr skill to create a new ADR. Ask for the decision title if not provided, then guide through context, options considered, decision made, and consequences.

design-review.description: Run a systematic pre-production design review
design-review.prompt: Use the design-review skill to conduct a thorough design review. Ask the user to describe the solution or share the design document, then work through the complete review checklist.

solution-doc.description: Generate architecture documentation — HLD, LLD, runbook, or deployment guide
solution-doc.prompt: Use the solution-doc skill. Ask which document type is needed: High-Level Design (HLD), Low-Level Design (LLD), deployment guide, or runbook. Then gather the required information and generate the document.

tech-stack.description: Evaluate technology options and recommend a stack
tech-stack.prompt: Use the tech-stack skill to systematically evaluate technology options. Ask the user to describe the problem context, constraints, and any options already under consideration, then run the evaluation framework.

cloud.description: Design cloud architecture or generate IaC (Terraform, CloudFormation, Bicep, CDK)
cloud.prompt: Use the cloud skill. Ask which cloud provider (AWS, Azure, GCP) and what is needed: a cloud architecture design, IaC code generation, a Well-Architected review, cost analysis, a migration plan, or a disaster recovery design.

capacity.description: Estimate capacity, design scaling strategy, or size infrastructure
capacity.prompt: Use the capacity skill. Ask what is needed: capacity estimation, a scaling strategy design, bottleneck analysis, load test planning, growth forecasting, or right-sizing existing infrastructure.
